_comment: RFC 3986 §6.2.2 percent/case-normalization vectors for app/federation/grammar.py:normalize_uri_component. RFC 3986 does not ship a separate machine-readable vector file; these are its WORKED EXAMPLES (verbatim, marked rfc-example) plus DIRECT applications of its normalization rules to the §2.3 unreserved set (marked rfc-rule-application). All are grounded in the RFC text — the spec author's examples or its explicit rules — NOT derived from PPR's implementation. See README.md for the source URL + sections. The verbatim examples anchor the reading (decode-unreserved §6.2.2.2; uppercase-hex §6.2.2.1); the rule-applications extend coverage under that anchored reading. normalize_uri_component is the LENIENT primitive (raw chars + malformed % are left verbatim); federation_id's stricter internal-id composes the same per-%XX step.

examples.source: RFC 3986 §6.2.2.1 — 'e.g., "%3a" versus "%3A"' hex digits normalized to uppercase ('::' is reserved, stays encoded)
RFC 3986 §6.2.2.1 (slash is reserved gen-delim — kept encoded, hex uppercased)
RFC 3986 §6.2.2.1 (non-ASCII octet — not unreserved, kept encoded, hex uppercased)
RFC 3986 §6.2.2.1/.2 mixed — UTF-8 octet kept+uppercased, %2D decoded to hyphen, raw chars verbatim
